productGroups.name: Halo Beacons
productGroups.notes: Separate beacons section with a learn-more link but no compare, compatibility, or quick-add affordance.

productGroups.name: Halo Accessories
productGroups.notes: Mixed families are presented in one flat grid, making compatibility scanning difficult.

productGroups.name: Halo Merch
productGroups.notes: Merch is visually equal to replacement parts even though purchase intent differs.

navFooterBehavior: Yellow promo strip rotates current savings and guarantee messages.
Navigation is sticky and uses the V3-style Shop Halo mega menu with Halo Collar product cards plus Beacons and Accessories side links.
Mobile navigation moves Shop Halo products and accessory links into a right-side drawer.
Footer is a dense multi-column link list covering Shop, About, Pack Membership, Support, and Why Halo pages.
A mini-cart drawer shell exists globally but the live accessories grid does not expose visible quick-add triggers.
datedIssuesToSolve: Flat storefront grid does not expose product family, compatibility, or purchase priority.
Product cards lack price, quick-add, compare, proof, and secondary details.
Beacons, parts, charging, and merch share the same card treatment despite different buying jobs.
No bundle entry point even though accessories are natural add-ons to a collar order.
No support/warranty cross-sell for replacement parts and compatibility questions.
Category density is low-value: visible product count is useful, but the shopping controls are missing.

v3CommercePrimitives: White commerce surface with Inter/system sans, black text, muted gray lines, and Halo yellow as the primary CTA color.
Sticky desktop right-rail buy stack paired with a media/gallery column.
Desktop waterfall gallery and mobile active-image gallery with thumbnail strip.
Rounded 8-16px product media containers, clean cards, and compact proof badges.
Selection cards expose active, expanded, and added states through border and background changes.
Price stack uses current price, struck list price, sale/savings copy, and full-width yellow add-to-cart CTA.
Mini-cart slides from the right with overlay, item rows, quantity controls, totals, promo disclosure, and Escape/overlay close.
Mobile purchase affordance keeps price and CTA close to the shopping decision without copying the full collar configurator.
